deck_goal: Recommend a grounded China EV market-entry plan for Europe and Southeast Asia.
audience: Regional strategy leaders deciding 2025 market sequencing

slides.title: China EV Market Entry: Europe & Southeast Asia Strategy
slides.goal: Introduce the deck purpose and frame the strategic question for regional decision-makers
key_points: Recommend a China EV market-entry plan targeting Europe and Southeast Asia
Support 2025 market sequencing decisions for regional strategy leaders
visual_elements: Main title with deck goal statement
Subtitle identifying audience: Regional strategy leaders
Clean, professional cover with minimal decorative elements
slides.speaker_notes: This deck synthesizes evidence from market tracking, entry playbooks, and cost benchmarks to recommend a grounded 2025 sequencing plan. The audience is regional strategy leaders responsible for execution decisions.

slides.title: 出口时间线 (Export Timeline)
slides.goal: Establish the chronological evolution of China EV export strategy from 2022 to 2024
key_points: 2022: 试点市场 (pilot market) phase
2023: Formation of dual-track strategy — Europe and Southeast Asia
2024: Channel deepening by region
核心结论: 2022-2024 年，中国新能源汽车出口节奏已经从单点放量转向分区域深化
visual_elements: Horizontal timeline with three phases: 2022 → 2023 → 2024
Phase labels in zh-CN with brief descriptors
Arrow showing directional shift from single-point to regional deepening
Source citation at bottom: src-01:sec-01
slides.speaker_notes: The export trajectory from 2022 to 2024 shows a clear maturation. What began as single-point pilots in 2022 evolved into a structured dual-track approach by 2023, with 2024 marking the shift toward region-specific channel deepening. This context sets the foundation for our 2025 sequencing recommendation.

slides.title: 欧洲 vs 东南亚对比 (Europe vs Southeast Asia Comparison)
slides.goal: Surface the strategic tradeoffs between Europe and Southeast Asia market entry
key_points: Europe: Higher unit revenue, stronger brand elevation potential, but heavier certification, tariff, and after-sales compliance burden
Southeast Asia: Faster channel establishment, more responsive local partners
核心结论: 欧洲适合追求品牌抬升与高客单价，东南亚更适合追求进入速度与渠道复制
visual_elements: Two-column comparison layout: Europe | Southeast Asia
Criteria rows: Unit revenue, Brand value, Channel speed, Regulatory burden, Partner responsiveness
Visual icons or color coding for strengths/weaknesses per column
Source citation: src-01:sec-02
slides.speaker_notes: This comparison clarifies the fundamental tradeoff. Europe offers higher margins and brand positioning but demands upfront investment in compliance and after-sales infrastructure. Southeast Asia trades lower unit economics for speed and operational flexibility. The strategic fit depends on whether the priority is brand building or market velocity.

slides.title: 进入路径 (Entry Path)
slides.goal: Present the recommended stepwise market entry sequence for 2025
key_points: Step 1: Establish distribution, service, and spare parts response in Southeast Asia
Step 2: Validate operational model in Southeast Asia market conditions
Step 3: Migrate validated model to priority European countries
核心结论: 先在东南亚建立渠道和售后，再择机进入欧洲，是更稳妥的 2025 市场进入路径
visual_elements: Sequential process flow: Southeast Asia (Step 1 & 2) → Migration Arrow → Europe (Step 3)
Step boxes with icons for distribution, service, spare parts
Decision gate indicator for timing of Europe entry
Source citation: src-02:sec-01
slides.speaker_notes: The entry playbook recommends Southeast Asia first. Building distribution, service, and spare parts capability in a more responsive market allows operational validation before confronting European compliance complexity. The model is then transferred to Europe once confidence is established. This staged approach reduces execution risk in 2025.

slides.title: 成本与利润指标 (Cost & Margin Indicators)
slides.goal: Present battery cost reduction as an enabling buffer for pricing and channel investment
key_points: Trailing 12-month battery pack cost reduction: 18%
This cost buffer enables: competitive pricing in target markets, channel subsidies, after-sales investment
核心结论: 18% 的电池成本降幅为价格带竞争和渠道投入提供了缓冲空间
visual_elements: Bar chart or waterfall showing 18% cost reduction with before/after metrics
Three downstream benefit callouts: pricing competitiveness, channel subsidies, after-sales investment
Percentage callout prominently displayed
Source citation: src-03:sec-01
slides.speaker_notes: The 18% battery cost reduction over the past 12 months is a significant enabler. It provides financial headroom for aggressive pricing in competitive markets, margin for channel partner subsidies, and investment capacity in after-sales infrastructure. This cost buffer supports both the Southeast Asia speed-first and Europe quality-first strategies.

slides.title: Decision Backbone
slides.goal: Compress minimum grounded claims into a decision-ready summary for strategy leaders
key_points: China EV exports have shifted from single-point to regional deepening (2022-2024)
Europe favors brand elevation and high unit economics; Southeast Asia favors speed and channel replication
Recommended 2025 path: Build and validate in Southeast Asia first, then migrate to Europe
18% battery cost reduction provides pricing and channel investment buffer
visual_elements: Four concise bullet points, each grounded to a source unit
Source tag next to each bullet (src-01:sec-01, src-01:sec-02, src-02:sec-01, src-03:sec-01)
Clean layout with clear visual hierarchy for rapid executive consumption
slides.speaker_notes: These four grounded claims form the minimum evidence base for the 2025 sequencing recommendation. They are directly sourced and auditable. Strategy leaders can reference this backbone to anchor subsequent detailed discussions or implementation planning.
